payload.message:
A4: House rules: Phones on silent, in pockets. No talking during the music - you can whisper to order, but the etiquette is clear. Requests only on Thursday DJ nights. We have a small library of listening journals where people can write about what they heard. No reservations for regular nights - first come, first served. But you can book the whole space for private listening sessions.

A5: Eight cocktails, each named after a tempo marking. Andante (slow gin drink, CHF 18), Allegro (bright citrus, CHF 18), Adagio (dark rum, contemplative, CHF 20), and so on. The signature is 'The Flip Side' - two drinks served together, one bright, one dark, meant to mirror the A and B side of a record. CHF 34 for the pair.

A6: We're taking over a former watchmaker's workshop. Original wood floors, worn beautifully. The turntable setup is central - a McIntosh system worth more than my car. Seating is a mix of vintage leather armchairs and a long listening bench along one wall. Lighting is warm and low - mostly brass lamps. The bar is small, almost an afterthought. The first thing people notice is the quiet. Then the speakers.
